key_insight: A hybrid model that uses sharp-interface heat transfer and diffuse-interface flow captures laser-induced melt pool dynamics much more accurately by preserving the extreme temperature gradients that drive evaporation and surface tension forces.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable simulation of laser welding and laser powder bed fusion, where small temperature errors can cause large mistakes in recoil pressure, Marangoni flow, and melt pool shape predictions.
method: Solve temperature with a sharp-interface unfitted finite element (CutFEM) formulation for accurate interface heat transfer.
Solve the multiphase flow with a robust level-set one-fluid diffuse-interface finite element model.
Extend the sharp-interface temperature into a thin interface band to compute temperature-dependent interface forces consistently.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports second-order spatial convergence for the thermal model, two orders of magnitude larger elements for 1% accuracy than a diffuse-interface approach, and about 10x higher accuracy than a purely diffuse model in a coupled benchmark.
watch_item: The approach depends on careful coupling between the sharp thermal field and diffuse flow interface, so implementation complexity and consistent force mapping are important practical concerns.
paper_title: A hybrid sharp-diffuse interface approach to accurately model melt pool dynamics with rapid evaporation in laser-based processing of metals
